titleStmt.title: Letter from Henry Wilson to James Craig, 1922
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Henry Wilson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland
msIdentifier.collection: Correspondence and papers of Field Marshall Sir Henry Wilson, PRONI/CAB-6-89-151

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
CAB6/89/15/ 1222 My dear P.M. Back from Paris & I find
your letter. I have just sent
you a wire to say I will
come over as soon as I can.
I am situated thus; The Army
Estimation are brought in on
Wed: so are so mad & bad I
have promised to try and ?
them. I can come the following
day via Swinford arriving Friday
morning. Will that do?
If it wasn't for Wednesday I would
cross tonight but I think for what
I cam usefully implow the
days between now and Thursday
in making some plans.
Unfortunately there is no way
of getting this letter to you before
Thursday moring; I have been
down to the District PO but
there is now no Sunday post
for Ireland. Will you let me have a wire or
letter if this suits you.
You can wire Henry
36 Eaton Place & this will
do quite well. H
